response:
Phase one needs ten hours while phase two needs twenty.
Phase three requires forty hours and phase four needs eighty.
Phase five takes one hundred sixty hours to finish.
The sum of all five phases equals three hundred ten.
Each phase now takes fifty percent more time than planned.
The original total duration was twenty weeks for all work.
New duration becomes thirty weeks when time grows by half.
Order of phases stays the same despite the delay.
No extra resources were added to fix this issue.
Delays may impact future projects if not managed well.
Team leaders must adjust their schedules to fit new times.
Strict plans often fail when unexpected events occur.
Fortune favors those who adapt quickly to changes.
